---
title: "workr 1.1.0, by example"
subtitle: "A runnable tour of project orchestration, hooks, discovery, and CI artifacts"
---

[{workr}](https://github.com/Gilead-BioStats/workr) models a data pipeline as an
ordered list of function calls described in YAML. Version **1.1.0** adds the
pieces that turn a folder of those workflows into a real, scheduled, restartable
production run. This site demonstrates each addition with code you can copy and
outputs generated live when the page was built.

Everything here runs on base-R data (`datasets::ToothGrowth`) and SQL steps, so
it reproduces anywhere.

## The pipeline used throughout

```
workflows/
├── 01_ingest/     Growth.yaml           load ToothGrowth
├── 02_analyze/    Prepared → Summary → Flagged
│                  ZScore.yaml  (Active: false → skipped)
└── 03_report/     _config.yaml + StudyReport.yaml
```

Data flows **RAW → prepared → summarized → flagged → report**, one YAML step per
transformation.

## What each page shows

| Page | Feature |
|---|---|
| [RunProject](project.qmd) | Multi-phase orchestration + per-phase `_config.yaml` |
| [Hooks](hooks.qmd) | Load/save hooks that decouple storage from logic |
| [Discovery](discovery.qmd) | Active/inactive, priority, meta-filtering, exact match |
| [Continue-on-error](continue-on-error.qmd) | Record failures and keep running |
| [CI artifacts](ci.qmd) | Pass workflow data between GitHub Actions runs |

## Run it yourself

```r
source("setup.R")                 # installs workr from GitHub
```

```sh
Rscript scripts/01_run_project.R
Rscript scripts/02_hooks.R
Rscript scripts/03_discovery.R
Rscript scripts/04_continue_on_error.R
```
